issues_found: Causal overclaiming from observational cross-sectional data. Paper uses 'causally mediates,' 'causal chain,' and 'causal status of absorption' based on Baron-Kenny mediation from 48 cross-sectional observations. The paper's own within-width matching null (Table 4) shows Gamma=1.0 for ALL metrics under all three within-width strategies (4, 23, and 16 pairs). The causal evidence vanishes when width is held constant by design. An alternative common-cause explanation (SAE feature quality drives both low absorption and high quality) is consistent with all data and indistinguishable from mediation in this design.

Table 3 mixes standardized and unstandardized coefficients without consistent labeling. Paths a,b labeled '(std)' match standardized values (P1_mediation.json: path_a_std, path_b_std). Direct effect c' for SCR = -0.029 (standardized: -0.0291) in Table 3 but -0.003 (unstandardized: -0.00290) in abstract and Section 5.1. Indirect effect ab = 0.025 matches unstandardized (0.02469) not standardized (0.2478). Arithmetic check a*b = ab FAILS across coefficient types. Any reviewer checking this will immediately identify the error.

H2 cross-domain absorption classified as 'PARTIALLY_SUPPORTED' in abstract, final_results.json, and final_results_summary.md. By the paper's own pre-registered falsification criterion (absorption must exceed 3x shuffled baseline), H2 is falsified: shuffled baseline = 100%, so 3x threshold impossible. Cosine-calibrated metric detects 0% across all thresholds. Dominance-based absorption rate equals false-negative rate at tau_dom=1.0 (confirmed: Table 5 R_abs column = FN Rate column in every row). Paper body (Section 4.2) correctly describes the result but framing metadata contradicts it.

Data pipeline integrity failure: final_results.json reports WRONG values for 4+ fields. (1) H5 corrected_rate=0.923 (actual: 0.192 from P4_taxonomy_correction.json), delta=0.0 (actual: -0.731), verdict='CORRECTION_MINIMAL' (should be 'CORRECTION_LARGE'). (2) All four Sobel z values from alternative_direction_test (reverse path Quality->Absorption->L0) not primary mediation path: TPP z=2.632 (should be 2.083, p=0.037), SCR z=3.886 (should be 3.621, p=0.00029), SP-F1 z=4.334 (should be 4.083, p=4.4e-5), Unlearning z=0.449 (should be from primary path). (3) n_full_mediations=0 (should be 2: SCR + TPP both satisfy all Baron-Kenny steps). (4) phase_boundary_detected=false (P3_scaling_surface.json source: true). Root cause: integration code reads from results_by_metric[metric].alternative_direction_test instead of results_by_metric[metric].sobel_test, and fails to propagate Phase 4 corrected values.

Phase 2 conducted on GPT-2 Small (124M) with 24k SAE having 98% dead features on city prompts. Original plan: Gemma 2 2B with Gemma Scope SAEs (16k-1M features). GPT-2 Small has ~500 live features for 3,552 cities across 5 attribute types -- categorically incapable of testing cross-domain absorption. The 0% cosine-calibrated rate is an inevitable consequence of the model choice. Abstract and Contribution 2 framing still implies the experiment answers the cross-domain question.

Phase 3 GAM (420 SAEs) omits architecture as covariate despite 360 standard (L1/TopK from sae_bench) + 54 JumpReLU (Gemma Scope) + 6 unknown spanning different width/L0 ranges. JumpReLU uses different sparsity mechanism (trained L0 via straight-through estimator) and is concentrated at specific width-L0 points. The width-L0 interaction term may partly capture architecture differences rather than genuine interaction physics.

SP-F1 suppression effect (partial r strengthening from -0.664 to -0.746) is presented as 'central finding' but interpretation is fragile. SP-F1 total effect c is NON-SIGNIFICANT (p=0.45) -- Baron-Kenny step 3 fails. This is 'indirect-only mediation' per Zhao et al. (2010), weaker than classical mediation. A competing explanation is equally valid: L0 has a direct beneficial effect on SP-F1 through better reconstruction (c'=-0.270, p=0.001) partially canceling the harmful indirect effect via absorption. The paper acknowledges this in one sentence but does not give proportional treatment. By contrast, SCR shows full mediation where all Baron-Kenny steps are satisfied.

No multiple comparison correction despite 50+ tests on n=48 SAEs: 4 metrics x (partial correlation + 3 width-stratified strata + mediation Sobel + 5 Rosenbaum matching strategies). At alpha=0.05, 2-3 false positives expected by chance. TPP partial r (p=0.022) and TPP Sobel z (p=0.037) are borderline. SP-F1 (p=1.2e-9) and SCR (p=6.6e-5) will survive any reasonable correction.

Rosenbaum Gamma=2.65 (Mahalanobis matching, 17 pairs) vs Gamma=1.0 (all three within-width strategies: exact-width 4 pairs, median 23 pairs, tertile 16 pairs) is not adequately problematized. Mahalanobis matching on log(width), layer, log(L0) allows cross-width matches. Since absorption is strongly confounded with width, cross-width matched pairs may match on L0 but still differ substantially on width-correlated features, inflating Gamma.

Figure numbering jumps from 3 to 5 (no Figure 4). Sections 4.3 (gradient analysis with phase boundary detection) and 4.4 (taxonomy correction) have zero visual support. Three generated figures (fig4_hypothesis_verdicts, fig6_taxonomy_correction, fig7_crossdomain_by_layer) are not referenced in paper text. Section 4.3 reports '443 points with gradient magnitude exceeding the 70th percentile threshold (0.69), with maximum gradient magnitude of 0.99' entirely in text without visualization. 'Figures and Tables' section after Conclusion lists raw filenames (fig1_partial_correlations.pdf) -- production artifact.
issues_fixed: ITER4-EXP001 (FIXED): H3 width-L0 confound fully resolved. Iteration 5 redesigned Phase 1 to add L0 as covariate in partial correlations, compute within-width-tier correlations, and perform suppressor diagnosis. 3/4 metrics retain |partial_r| > 0.2 after L0 control. SP-F1 shows suppression effect (strengthening from -0.664 to -0.746). This was the primary blocker from iter_004.

ITER4-EXP002 (FIXED): 92.3% taxonomy rate audited. Phase 4 taxonomy correction directly addresses the inflated rate, reducing to 19.2% via frequency-matched comparison baselines. Paper body correctly reports 19.2% (though final_results.json has the wrong value -- see EXP002).

ITER4-EXP006 (FIXED): PMI regression now uses letter-level clustered SEs (26 clusters). L0 significance drops from p=0.012 (HC3) to p=0.206 (clustered), revealing the HC3 result was an artifact of correlated residuals. Hurdle model and beta regression also reported.

ITER4-WRI001 (FIXED): 'Validated quality indicator' language replaced with 'quality-correlated' and 'quality predictor' throughout. Explicit acknowledgment that causal evidence requires intervention experiments.

ITER4-WRI003 (FIXED): Figure generation completed during experiment phase. 7 PNG + 4 PDF figures generated, 4 included in paper. This resolves the chronic 3-iteration figure generation lag (iter_001-003 had zero generated figures).

ITER4-PLAN001 (FIXED): Confound control analyses pre-specified in experimental plan as explicit tasks. The four-method convergent design (partial correlation, stratification, mediation, Rosenbaum) was planned from the start, not discovered in post-analysis.
ITER4-EXP005 (PARTIALLY FIXED): Cross-model gap managed through explicit model separation per phase. But GPT-2 Small fallback undermines Phase 2 validity.
PILOT-TO-FULL (FIXED): All 14 iter_005 experiments ran at FULL scale with mode='FULL'. This resolves the 3-iteration systemic PILOT mode blocker (iter_001-003).
ITER4-WRI002 (PARTIALLY FIXED): Title no longer references failed LV competitive exclusion framework.
